item.title: Academic
item.description: The highly flexible website framework for Hugo with an extensible plugin mechanism. Create a beautifully simple site in under 10 minutes 🚀 Latest release Star

item.title: Custom Section
item.description: This is an example of using the custom widget to create your own homepage section. To remove this section, either delete content/home/teaching.md or edit the frontmatter of the file to deactivate the widget by setting active = false.

item.title: Solving Multiagent Planning Problems with Concurrent Conditional Effects
CARPooL: Collective Adaptation using concuRrent PLanning
Collective Adaptation through Concurrent Planning: the Case of Sustainable Urban Mobility
Forward-Search Temporal Planning with Simultaneous Events
Solving Concurrent Multiagent Planning using Classical Planning
Resolution of Concurrent Planning Problems using Classical Planning
Early Experiences in Graphic Design
item.description: This time I’m going to talk about a kind of spin-off of my previous post, where I explained some experiences in the PSP scene. The parallel experience consisted in learning how to use design programs like Adobe Photoshop and GIMP. Here is the explanation of how I started designing signatures in forums! As soon as I had my PSP hacked, my participation in forums began to increase. I had previously visited some of them but just as a visitor, especially in ESPALPSP as I explained in the first post related to these “adventures”.

item.title: Adventures in the PSP Scene
item.description: In this post I’m going to explain my experiences in the PSP scene. The PSP scene opened many doors for me: meet new people, learn to use design programs, start programming for first time in my life, … Definitely, what I lived from 2007 to 2010 thanks to the PSP community was incredibly helpful for me from then on. I’ve divided in several chapters the whole content. You can access these chapters from the table of contents below.

item.title: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN) using Arduino and XBee
item.description: I have decided to write my first real post. I’m going to briefly write about a bunch of projects related to Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN) using Arduino boards and XBee modules. During my 3rd year in the Computer Science degree (2013 - 2014), I coursed a subject called Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN). My teammates, Sergio Barrachina Muñoz and Francesc Wilhelmi Roca, and me did some projects during this course. During the course, we used an Arduino board (which now outside USA is called Genuino) together with some electronic components (e.

item.title: Welcome to my blog!
item.description: Hi everyone! After some months (and even years) thinking about starting my own webpage, I have finally done it. The main reason for which I did not do it before was that I thought I could not explain anything interesting… and maybe I cannot! However, I wanted to have a place where I could explain experiences, as well as things I have learnt, things I am learning, and things I will (hopefully) learn.

item.title: Learning and Generalization in Atari Games
